refined_fr.refined_fr: CR-NGHE-003 Refined: The NGHE solution shall enable operators and tele-operators to achieve a measured energy efficiency of energy_per_ton ≤ E_target (customer-defined threshold) over the agreed evaluation period, through integrated monitoring, control optimization, and real-time feedback mechanisms.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is outcome-focused but lacks explicit linkage to the mechanisms (monitoring, control, feedback) that enable efficiency achievement. Refinement clarifies that energy efficiency is not a passive property but an active capability requiring measurement, visibility, and operator decision support. This aligns with the high safety criticality constraint by ensuring operators have visibility into energy consumption patterns.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure energy_per_ton = (total_energy_consumed_kWh / total_mass_moved_tons) across a defined evaluation period (e.g., 40-hour work week). Verify that measured value ≤ E_target. Collect telemetry from Vehicle power systems and cross-validate with site energy meters. Confirm operator feedback mechanisms are functional and used.
sub_frs: CR-NGHE-003.1: The NGHE solution shall continuously measure and log energy consumption (kWh) at the Vehicle level.
CR-NGHE-003.2: The NGHE solution shall continuously measure and log mass moved (tons) at the Vehicle level.
CR-NGHE-003.3: The NGHE solution shall compute and display energy_per_ton KPI in real-time to the Operator and Tele-operator.
CR-NGHE-003.4: The NGHE solution shall provide predictive or advisory feedback to optimize energy efficiency without compromising safety.
CR-NGHE-003.5: The NGHE solution shall log energy efficiency data for post-evaluation analysis and reporting to Site Manager.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Implement energy monitoring at Vehicle power subsystem; compute KPI locally on Vehicle; transmit telemetry to Teleoperation for operator visibility; store logs in Vehicle onboard storage.

DP-B: Implement energy monitoring at Vehicle power subsystem; compute KPI on a centralized cloud backend; transmit raw telemetry via Network Link; provide real-time KPI feedback via Telemetry interface to both Operator and Tele-operator.

DP-C: Implement dual energy monitoring (Vehicle onboard + external site meter); compute KPI via hybrid local-cloud architecture; use Safety Services to validate energy efficiency compliance; provide feedback via Telemetry and SafetyAlert interfaces.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-B: Implement energy monitoring at Vehicle power subsystem; compute KPI on a centralized backend; transmit raw telemetry via Network Link; provide real-time KPI feedback via Telemetry interface to both Operator and Tele-operator.

potential_sources: DP-B couples energy monitoring (Vehicle domain) with KPI computation (backend domain) via Network Link. This is a controlled, well-defined coupling through a known interface.
DP-B decouples operator feedback from Vehicle control logic, reducing safety-critical complexity on the Vehicle.
DP-B enables both local (Operator) and remote (Tele-operator) visibility without duplicating computation logic.
coupling_check.notes: DP-B avoids the complexity of DP-C (dual monitoring, Safety Services integration) while maintaining the visibility and feedback benefits. DP-A is rejected because local-only computation limits tele-operator visibility and complicates post-evaluation analysis.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Network Link is reliable and low-latency enough to support real-time KPI feedback without safety impact.
Assumption: Vehicle power subsystem can provide accurate, continuous energy consumption telemetry.
Risk: If Network Link fails, KPI feedback is unavailable; mitigation: implement local fallback display on Vehicle.
Risk: Backend KPI computation introduces latency; mitigation: define acceptable latency threshold (e.g., <5 sec) and validate in testing.

Risk: High safety criticality requires that energy efficiency feedback does not interfere with safety-critical control loops; mitigation: segregate KPI computation from safety-critical Vehicle control.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: DP-B is sound. Recommend adding explicit requirement that KPI computation and feedback mechanisms are non-safety-critical and do not block or delay safety-critical Vehicle operations.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution IM is the top-level system boundary that integrates Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. For CR-NGHE-003, the NGHE Solution IM owns the end-to-end energy efficiency capability: it orchestrates energy monitoring (Vehicle), KPI computation (backend), and feedback delivery (Teleoperation + Vehicle display).

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Vehicle transmits raw energy consumption and mass moved data via Telemetry (in). NGHE Solution backend computes KPI and transmits KPI feedback via Telemetry (out) to Operator and Tele-operator displays.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Vehicle and backend communicate via Network Link to exchange raw telemetry and KPI feedback. Network Link is the transport layer for Telemetry interface.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Site Manager and Tele-operator issue work tasking via Tasking interface. NGHE Solution uses task context (e.g., expected mass, duration) to set energy efficiency targets and validate KPI achievement.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: If energy efficiency degrades below acceptable threshold (e.g., energy_per_ton > E_target + margin), NGHE Solution may issue SafetyAlert to notify Safety Manager and operators of potential efficiency anomaly or equipment degradation.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the system-level boundary. CR-NGHE-003 is a system-level KPI that spans Vehicle (monitoring), backend (computation), and Teleoperation (feedback). Containment is high because all sub-components (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) are owned by NGHE Solution. Minimal diffusion: only Network Link and Telemetry interfaces cross the boundary.

Vehicle owns energy monitoring hardware and local telemetry transmission. However, KPI computation and feedback logic are external (backend). If energy efficiency logic were entirely on Vehicle, containment would be higher, but DP-B explicitly moves computation off-Vehicle for safety and tele-operator visibility. Diffusion: Vehicle must interface with backend via Network Link and Telemetry.

Teleoperation owns the tele-operator interface and feedback display. However, KPI computation and Vehicle monitoring are external. Teleoperation is a consumer of KPI data, not an owner of the energy efficiency capability. Diffusion: Teleoperation depends on Telemetry interface from backend.

Safety Services may validate energy efficiency compliance or issue alerts if efficiency degrades. However, Safety Services does not own the core energy monitoring or KPI computation. Role is advisory/monitoring only. Diffusion: Safety Services is loosely coupled via SafetyAlert interface.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: NGHE Solution is the correct IM boundary because CR-NGHE-003 is a system-level KPI that requires integration across Vehicle (data source), backend (computation), and Teleoperation (feedback). No single sub-module (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) can own the full capability. NGHE Solution orchestrates the end-to-end flow and ensures consistency.
containment_risks: Risk: If backend KPI computation is implemented as a separate microservice outside NGHE Solution ownership, diffusion increases and accountability becomes unclear. Mitigation: explicitly assign backend KPI computation to NGHE Solution or create a dedicated sub-module (e.g., 'Energy Efficiency Backend') owned by NGHE Solution.

Risk: Vehicle and Teleoperation may have conflicting energy efficiency targets or feedback logic. Mitigation: NGHE Solution must define and enforce a single, consistent energy efficiency policy across all interfaces.
Risk: Network Link failure isolates Vehicle from backend KPI computation. Mitigation: implement local fallback KPI display on Vehicle using cached E_target and local energy/mass data.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Assign CR-NGHE-003 to NGHE Solution IM. Recommend creating a sub-module 'Energy Efficiency Backend' (owned by NGHE Solution) to encapsulate KPI computation logic and reduce diffusion. Define explicit interface contracts for Telemetry (energy/mass data format) and SafetyAlert (efficiency threshold and alert format).

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-B (centralized KPI computation with real-time feedback) directly addresses the refined FR (enable operators to achieve energy_per_ton ≤ E_target). DP-B provides measurement, visibility, and feedback mechanisms required by the FR. No logical gaps or contradictions. DP-B is implementable and testable.

diffusion_risks: DP-B couples Vehicle (monitoring) with backend (computation) via Network Link. If Network Link is unreliable, KPI feedback is delayed or unavailable. Mitigation: implement local fallback display on Vehicle.

DP-B requires Telemetry interface to carry both raw energy/mass data (Vehicle → backend) and computed KPI (backend → Operator/Tele-operator). Interface must be bidirectional and well-defined. Mitigation: document Telemetry interface schema and latency requirements.

DP-B introduces backend computation as a new architectural component. If backend is not owned by NGHE Solution, accountability and integration risk increase. Mitigation: explicitly assign backend to NGHE Solution or create a sub-module.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution IM contains all necessary sub-modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) to realize DP-B. Containment is high (95%). Diffusion is low (5%) and limited to well-defined external interfaces (Network Link, Telemetry). No external dependencies on modules outside NGHE Solution.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: CR-NGHE-003 is verifiable via measurement: energy_per_ton = (total_energy_consumed / total_mass_moved) over evaluation period. Verification requires: (1) energy telemetry from Vehicle, (2) mass telemetry from Vehicle, (3) KPI computation and logging, (4) comparison against E_target. All are implementable within NGHE Solution IM.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: CR-NGHE-003 is allocated to NGHE Solution IM. Sub-FRs (CR-NGHE-003.1 through 003.5) are allocated to Vehicle (monitoring), backend (computation), Teleoperation (feedback), and Safety Services (validation). Allocation is clear and traceable.

recommended_revisions: Refine DP-B to explicitly define backend KPI computation logic (e.g., rolling average, real-time vs. batch, frequency of updates).
Define Telemetry interface schema: energy (kWh), mass (tons), timestamp, KPI (kWh/ton), confidence level.
Define Network Link latency and reliability requirements (e.g., <5 sec latency, 99.5% availability).
Define fallback behavior if Network Link fails: Vehicle displays local KPI estimate; backend queues telemetry for later sync.
Define SafetyAlert trigger: if energy_per_ton > E_target + margin (e.g., 10%) for >N consecutive measurements, issue alert to Safety Manager.

decision.justification: CR-NGHE-003 is not atomic. The refined FR has been decomposed into five sub-FRs (CR-NGHE-003.1 through 003.5) that address distinct functional concerns: energy measurement, mass measurement, KPI computation, feedback, and logging. Each sub-FR requires further decomposition to define DPs and IMs. DP-B and NGHE Solution IM are sound at the current level, but the sub-FRs must be refined and allocated to specific modules (Vehicle, backend, Teleoperation, Safety Services) before detailed design can proceed.
next_level_sub_frs: CR-NGHE-003.1: The NGHE solution shall continuously measure and log energy consumption (kWh) at the Vehicle level.
CR-NGHE-003.2: The NGHE solution shall continuously measure and log mass moved (tons) at the Vehicle level.
CR-NGHE-003.3: The NGHE solution shall compute and display energy_per_ton KPI in real-time to the Operator and Tele-operator.
CR-NGHE-003.4: The NGHE solution shall provide predictive or advisory feedback to optimize energy efficiency without compromising safety.
CR-NGHE-003.5: The NGHE solution shall log energy efficiency data for post-evaluation analysis and reporting to Site Manager.

assumptions: Network Link is reliable and low-latency (<5 sec) for real-time KPI feedback.
Vehicle power subsystem can provide accurate, continuous energy consumption telemetry.
Backend KPI computation is non-safety-critical and does not interfere with safety-critical Vehicle control.
E_target is defined by customer and provided as input to NGHE Solution at deployment time.
Evaluation period is agreed upon with customer (e.g., 40-hour work week) and is long enough to capture representative energy efficiency data.
open_risks: Network Link failure: KPI feedback unavailable; mitigation: implement local fallback display.
Backend computation latency: KPI feedback delayed; mitigation: define acceptable latency threshold and validate in testing.
Energy telemetry accuracy: measurement errors propagate to KPI; mitigation: calibrate Vehicle power sensors and validate against external meters.
Operator behavior: operators may not use KPI feedback effectively; mitigation: provide training and incentives for energy-efficient operation.

Safety interference: energy efficiency optimization logic may conflict with safety-critical control; mitigation: segregate KPI computation from safety-critical Vehicle control and validate via safety analysis.
evidence_needed: Vehicle power subsystem specification: energy measurement accuracy, sampling rate, telemetry format.
Network Link specification: latency, reliability, bandwidth, failure modes.
Backend KPI computation specification: algorithm, update frequency, error handling.
Telemetry interface specification: data schema, encoding, transport protocol.
Operator feedback interface specification: display format, update frequency, user interaction.
Safety analysis: confirmation that KPI computation and feedback do not introduce new safety hazards.
Evaluation plan: definition of E_target, evaluation period, measurement methodology, acceptance criteria.
Test plan: verification of energy measurement accuracy, KPI computation correctness, feedback latency, Network Link fallback behavior.
